claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your situation.

First, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children, and what are their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children. Alex is 3 years old, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 10.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have three children: Alex (3 years old), Blake (8 years old), and Charlie (10 years old).

Now I need to ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, not all of them. Alex and Charlie live with me, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I'll need to ask some questions about each child's situation.

Let me start with Blake, who doesn't live with you:

**Where does Blake live, and what is your relationship or responsibility towards Blake?** For example, does Blake live with another parent, a relative, or in care? And do you contribute towards Blake's upbringing in any way?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake lives with their other parent. I'm not currently able to contribute financially towards their upkeep at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

**Does Blake's other parent claim Child Benefit for Blake, or do you know if anyone else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, their other parent claims Child Benefit for Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Now, regarding Alex and Charlie who live with you:

**Do Alex and Charlie live with you full-time, or do they spend time living elsewhere (for example, with another parent or relative)?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a few more questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex and Charlie - meaning do you provide for their care and upbringing on a day-to-day basis?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I take care of them day-to-day and provide for their needs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. 

Just one final question to complete the assessment:

**Are either Alex or Charlie currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? Or are they in any kind of hospital care or local authority care for an extended period?**

(This mainly applies to older children, but I need to check for completeness)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not in any education or training programs yet - they're still quite young. And they're not in hospital care or local authority care either.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Complete

Based on all the information you've provided, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

**✅ You ARE eligible for Child Benefit for:**
- **Alex** (age 3)
- **Charlie** (age 10)

**❌ You are NOT eligible for Child Benefit for:**
- **Blake** (age 8)

### Explanation:

**For Alex and Charlie:** You meet all the eligibility requirements because:
- They are both under 16 years old
- They live with you full-time in the UK
- You are responsible for their day-to-day care and upbringing
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them

**For Blake:** Unfortunately, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake because:
- Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any one time
- Blake lives with their other parent, not with you

### Next Steps:
You can apply for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. You would typically receive Child Benefit for your eldest qualifying child at one rate, and additional amounts for each younger child.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time in the UK, you are responsible for their day-to-day care and upbringing, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old and lives with their other parent who is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at a time. Since Blake's other parent is claiming and you do not live with Blake or contribute to their upkeep, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 10 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time in the UK, you are responsible for their day-to-day care and upbringing, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children. They are eligible for Child Benefit for two of them (Alex and Charlie) who live with them full-time and for whom they provide day-to-day care. They are not eligible for Blake, who lives with the other parent who already claims Child Benefit for that child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time in the UK, you are responsible for their day-to-day care and upbringing, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old and lives with their other parent who is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at a time. Since Blake's other parent is claiming and you do not live with Blake or contribute to their upkeep, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 10 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time in the UK, you are responsible for their day-to-day care and upbringing, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children. They are eligible for Child Benefit for two of them (Alex and Charlie) who live with them full-time and for whom they provide day-to-day care. They are not eligible for Blake, who lives with the other parent who already claims Child Benefit for that child.
